meta_title: For Founders: deployment workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Deployment Workflow to Stop Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Master a high-velocity deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your SaaS MVP in record time.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of deployment momentum. By implementing a standardized CI/CD pipeline using Antigravity and Claude Code, you automate the bridge between local development and production. This removes friction, ensures continuous validation, and forces your MVP into the hands of real users faster.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is paved with 'almost-finished' codebases. You’ve spent weeks architecting the perfect database schema and tweaking UI components, yet your project remains trapped on your local machine, invisible to the market. This inertia isn't a lack of coding skill; it's a failure of your deployment architecture.

Real founders don't strive for perfection; they strive for feedback. By adopting a streamlined, automated deployment workflow, you transform the intimidating 'release' event into a non-event. It’s time to stop polishing pixels in isolation and start building a product that actually generates revenue.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Before touching infrastructure, define the one core feature that solves a burning problem. We use Antigravity to scaffold the minimal viable architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a clean SaaS MVP architecture for a [Insert Idea] app using Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind. Focus on a single user flow: authentication to core value delivery.

steps.title: Configure Automated CI/CD
steps.description: Remove the manual deployment tax. Automate your build process so every push to 'main' triggers a production-ready build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys to Vercel upon merging to main.

steps.title: Database Schema Synchronization
steps.description: Stop manual database migrations. Use Claude Code to ensure your local schema matches your production environment automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a migration strategy for Supabase that keeps local dev and production schemas in sync without data loss.

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging
steps.description: Deploy unfinished features safely to production behind flags, allowing you to ship daily without breaking the user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a lightweight feature flagging system into the current Next.js codebase to allow controlled feature rollouts.

steps.title: Automated Smoke Testing
steps.description: Ensure your critical path always works. Use Claude Code to generate end-to-end tests that run before every deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Playwright tests for the critical user onboarding flow to ensure no regression during deployment.

pro_tips: Ship on Fridays: If it breaks, you fix it over the weekend; if it works, you have a live product for Monday morning.
Database First: Never write UI code until the database schema for that feature is defined and migrated.
The 80/20 Rule: If a feature takes more than 4 hours to deploy, cut it or simplify the scope.
Log Everything: Use an observability tool like Axiom or Sentry from day one to see how users break your app.
Delete Code: If a feature hasn't been used in 30 days, delete it. Bloat is the silent killer of project momentum.
content.next_steps: Once your deployment pipeline is live, set a 'Public Launch' date exactly 14 days from today. Use the remaining time to polish only the core user journey and gather your first 10 beta testers.
content.conversion_block: Don't let your code gather digital dust. The market is waiting for your solution, and the only way to win is to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
